name: Clarifi - Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Delaware Valley
description_short: Non-profit founded 50+ years ago serving Greater Philadelphia with free financial counseling, housing services, and direct assistance for low- and moderate-income residents of PA, NJ, and DE.
description_long:
Clarifi, formerly known as Consumer Credit Counseling Service of Delaware Valley, is a Philadelphia-based non-profit with over five decades of operation. It is Greater Philadelphia's largest provider of financial and housing empowerment services, focusing on low- and moderate-income residents across Southeastern Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and Delaware. The organization is built around the belief that everyone deserves judgment-free access to financial resources tailored to their specific situation and goals.

Clarifi offers a broad suite of services organized around four pillars: certified financial empowerment counseling, housing success programs, benefits access assistance, and direct financial assistance. Their counseling covers budgeting, debt management, and credit issues, while their housing programs include homebuyer education, foreclosure prevention, and a notable home repair program that secured $6.9 million for clients in 2024 alone. They also disburse grants directly to clients — $1.3 million in 2024 — and help households access government and community benefits they may not know they qualify for.

What distinguishes Clarifi from generic online credit counseling services is its deep regional embeddedness and measurable community impact. In 2024, the organization completed 4,911 counseling sessions across 3,657 households, helped clients achieve over 5,100 milestones, and reduced average client debt by $2,978. The home repair program in particular is unusual for a financial counseling organization and reflects a holistic understanding that housing stability and financial stability are inseparable for many low-income families.

For eligible residents of the Philadelphia region, Clarifi represents one of the strongest free resources available. The organization's non-profit status and HUD-approved credentials mean advice is not product-driven. The main limitations are geographic — services are restricted to Southeastern PA, NJ, and DE — and the website does not clearly publish fees, eligibility income thresholds, or waitlist information, which may require a phone call to assess fit.
pros: 50+ years of operation — one of the most established non-profits in the region
Greater Philadelphia's largest financial and housing empowerment provider
Secured $6.9 million in home repair funding for clients in 2024
Disbursed $1.3 million in direct grants to clients in 2024
Average client debt reduced by $2,978 per household
Serves all three states: Pennsylvania, New Jersey, and Delaware
Explicitly judgment-free model tailored to individual circumstances
cons: Geographic restriction — only serves Southeastern PA, NJ, and DE; no service for other states
Website does not publish fee schedules, income eligibility thresholds, or waitlist status
Scale limitation — 3,657 households counseled in 2024 is small relative to regional need
Service depth varies; home repair and direct grant programs may have separate eligibility requirements not disclosed online
best_for: Low- and moderate-income residents of the Greater Philadelphia region needing debt or budget counseling
Philadelphia-area homeowners facing foreclosure or needing affordable home repair financing
Households seeking to understand and access government or community benefits
First-time homebuyers in Southeastern PA, NJ, or DE wanting HUD-approved pre-purchase counseling
diagnosis: Clarifi is best for low- and moderate-income residents of Southeastern PA, NJ, or DE who need free, accredited financial or housing counseling from a long-established non-profit with a strong track record. The main caveat is strict geography — consumers outside the Philadelphia metro area need to look elsewhere — and the website lacks upfront detail on eligibility or fees, requiring direct contact to confirm service availability.
